articles.title: ‘A gift of mobility’: Jaishankar launches Jaipur Foot Centre, agro-hub in Trinidad and Tobago
articles.description: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar inaugurated an India-backed agro-processing facility in South Trinidad and launched a permanent Jaipur Foot Prosthetics Centre in Penal with Trinidad and Tobago Prime Minister Kamla Persad-Bissessar on May 10. The projects expand India’s development partnership in the Caribbean following Jaishankar’s diplomatic visits to Jamaica and Suriname and continue initiatives linked to Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s outreach to Trinidad and Tobago.

articles.title: Mother’s Day: What multi-cap investing borrows from a mother’s wisdom
articles.description: Multi Cap investing offers a structured approach to wealth creation. It mirrors a mother's wisdom by balancing immediate needs with long-term goals. This strategy allows participation across large, mid, and small-cap companies within a single framework. It provides meaningful diversification and helps investors stay invested across market cycles. Simplicity and consistency are key advantages for long-term success.

articles.title: Not Vijay, TMC, or BJP: Silent Gamblers Who Won ₹300 Crore | I Witness
articles.description: Prediction markets on Polymarket recorded nearly ₹300 crore in trading volume across India’s 2026 assembly elections, with individual bettors walking away with gains of up to ₹87 lakh. But here’s the catch: these markets aren’t legally allowed to operate in India. So how does it work, who’s placing these bets, and does collective wisdom actually get elections right? TOI’s Chandrima Banerjee breaks it down.

articles.title: Big Breaking: Yogi Cabinet Expansion Today in Lucknow
articles.description: Uttar Pradesh is set to witness a major political development today as Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath prepares for a cabinet expansion ahead of next year’s Assembly elections. The oath-taking ceremony for six new ministers will be held at 3:30 PM at Raj Bhavan in Lucknow, with all MLAs asked to arrive by 3 PM. Preparations for the ceremony are in full swing, and all existing ministers have also been invited. The leaders likely to take oath include former BJP state president and Jat leader Bhupendra Chaudhary, Brahmin face Manoj Pandey, Dalit leader Krishna Paswan, Surendra Diler, Hansraj Vishwakarma, and Kailash Rajput. The expansion is being viewed as BJP’s strategy to strengthen caste and regional equations before the crucial 2027 Assembly elections. Reports suggest that only vacant positions will be filled, with minimal reshuffling of existing ministries.

articles.title: Businesses and Thought Leaders Revolutionizing India in 2026
articles.description: New Delhi [India], May 9: India's growth story in 2026 is being shaped not only by large corporations, but also by visionary entrepreneurs, healthcare pioneers, sustainability advocates, researchers, and technology-driven changemakers who are redefining industries with innovation and purpose. From regulatory consulting and rural digital empowerment to green manufacturing, Ayurveda healthcare, compliance services, and advanced scientific research, a new wave of businesses and thought leaders is contributing to India's transformation across sectors. These organizations and individuals are driving meaningful impact through innovation, sustainability, community development, and future-focused solutions, reflecting the evolving spirit of a modern, self-reliant, and globally influential India.
